summary: DHH announces that Basecamp has made Firefox its primary browser target for development, with platform-specific browsers like Safari and IE as secondary. He personally still uses Safari on Mac for its speed and aesthetics but considers IE6 on Windows indefensible due to security issues. The post is a rallying cry for Firefox adoption, urging readers to switch and convert friends and family. An update highlights Microsoft's dismissal of Firefox as a threat, which DHH treats as hubris worth proving wrong.
key_points: Basecamp officially targets Firefox first for markup and design verification, treating platform browsers as secondary
DHH acknowledges Safari's superior feel on Mac but sees Firefox as the only sensible choice on Windows
Microsoft's public dismissal of Firefox is presented as corporate arrogance ripe for comeuppance
The post functions as grassroots advocacy, urging readers to evangelize Firefox to reach double-digit market share
money_quotes: Firefox is the primary browser target for Basecamp.
If I was still on a PC, I'd consider myself downright foolish if I was still using an out-dated and security issue-ridden browser like IE6.
Firefox is a great browser on any platform, on Windows it's the only sensible choice.
While you're at it, get friends and family on the wagon as well, and let's see Firefox climb to a two-digit market share in no time.

He claims that IE is just as secure as Firefox, that the Mozilla-based browser doesn't have any important features (otherwise MS customers would have told him!), and that it's no threat to the IE market share. Oh, please, please make him wrong.
key_insight: Backing an underdog open-source browser over the dominant incumbent was both a practical web standards decision and an ideological stand against complacent big tech.
